title: klickd Payload v4 (Strict GA candidate, normative)
description: Strict JSON Schema for the .klickd v4 inner payload. This schema is the GA strict equivalent of the permissive klickd-payload-v4-preview.schema.json. Strict blocks correspond to RFC-001 (media_profile v1, Accepted) and RFC-002 v1 core (verification_gates, human_veto_policy, claim_sources v1, Accepted). Sections that remain Draft (RFC-002 v2-additive: contract_tests, success_criteria, reversibility, blast_radius, verification_artifacts, error_journal.rule_created; RFC-004 migration runtime) are accepted but left permissive so they can be tightened by a future strict schema bump without breaking GA writers. Top-level additionalProperties is TRUE to preserve unknown fields verbatim (SPEC.md §33.7 forward-compatibility invariant). Strict shape on frozen sub-objects uses additionalProperties: false locally. Does NOT supersede the permissive preview schema (klickd-payload-v4-preview.schema.json) — both coexist until the preview sunset announced separately.

payload_schema_version.description: Payload schema version. For GA strict files, the canonical value is '4.0'. The preview value '4.0.0-preview.1' is also accepted so a preview file already in the wild round-trips against the strict schema without forcing a rewrite. A future strict bump (4.1, etc.) MAY tighten this enum.

domain_schema_version.description: Domain-specific schema version. Two accepted forms: (a) v3-style '{domain}-{major}.{minor}' (e.g. 'education-1.2'); (b) bare semver-ish '{major}.{minor}(.{patch})?' for domain-agnostic profiles (e.g. '1.0.0').

preview.description: OPTIONAL preview marker. Present only on files written against a preview iteration (e.g. 'v4.0.0-preview.1'). Absent on GA strict files.

profile_kind.description: Top-level discriminator for the profile shape. Reserved canonical values: 'learner', 'agent', 'team', 'robot', 'creator'. Custom strings are permitted (extension space). v3.x is implicitly 'learner'.

injection_target.description: Where to inject the .klickd context (carried over from v3.x §20.2).

identity.description: Persistent user identity attributes. Carried over from v3 SPEC §8; same strictness as v3 payload schema. All sub-fields are OPTIONAL.

display_name.description: v4 GA preferred user-facing alias. Either 'name' or 'display_name' may be used; producers SHOULD pick one.

companion_identity.description: Optional companion / co-agent persona. Same surface as v3.4 §27. Permissive.

context.description: Operational session state. Carried over from v3 SPEC §9. Permissive (unevaluatedProperties policy of v3 retained).

knowledge.description: Structured knowledge state. Carried over from v3 SPEC §10. Permissive.

memory.description: Structured conversation/event log. Same v3 §11 constraints retained.

learning_goal.description: User's stated learning / delivery goal. Same surface as v3 §27. Permissive.

user_preferences.description: Canonical type is string (v3.5 §22.6). Object form retained for backward compatibility.

agent_instructions.description: System-prompt injection string. Same v3 §7.2 contract.

ethics.description: Ethical guard surface (v3.x). Permissive in this schema; safety / locked fields are governed by docs/spec/DEPRECATION_POLICY_V4.md §9.

media_profile.description: RFC-001 v1 (Accepted): portable, hash-referenced media context. Strict on the v1 frozen surface (versioned + entries[]); a permissive 'summary' form (preview-era) is also accepted for round-trip compatibility. New modalities / fields land via additive RFC bumps.

version.description: media_profile schema version. v1 per RFC-001.

oneOf.description: Permissive summary form (preview-era). Strict producers SHOULD migrate to the versioned + entries[] form. Kept for round-trip parity with vectors_v40_preview.json.

verification_gates.description: RFC-002 v1 core (Accepted): user's preferred friction profile per action class. Two accepted shapes: (a) structured form (recommended) with version + gates[]; (b) flat map {action_class: level} for compactness (used by §33.5 minimal example and preview readers).

oneOf.description: Flat map form: {action_class: gate_level}. Each value MUST be one of the five canonical levels.

human_veto_policy.description: RFC-002 v1 (Accepted): standing rules about when a human MUST be in the loop. Overrides any lower gate.

applies_to.description: List of action classes for which a human veto is required.

second_party.description: Optional named second party who may unlock. null means owner-only.

min_level.description: Floor gate level. The policy MUST NOT resolve below this level for any action class in 'applies_to'. RFC-002 v1 §4 #6 — human veto is sacred.

claim_sources.description: RFC-002 v1 (Accepted) + v2-additive (Draft). Strict on v1 fields ('prefer', 'require_citation_for'); 'records' and extended fields remain permissive (v2 still Draft).

prefer.description: Ordered preference list. Each entry is a free-form source identifier; common values: 'user_supplied', 'tool:web_search', 'tool:doi_resolver'.

risk_thresholds.description: RFC-002 v1 (Accepted): numeric / categorical knobs. Permissive on inner shape — extension space.

preflight_checks.description: RFC-002 v1 (Accepted): small named checks to run before acting. Each entry is an object with at minimum 'name'.

error_journal.description: RFC-002 v1 (Accepted): append-only lessons learned. Permissive on entry shape ('rule_created' remains Draft in v2-additive).

verification_artifacts.description: RFC-002 §8b.8 (Draft, v2-additive). Permissive: pointer ledger, not a payload sink. Accepted as null or array.

reversibility.description: RFC-002 v2-additive (Draft). Permissive — typical shape is {action_class: enum<reversible|costly_to_reverse|irreversible|low|none|high>} but enums are not yet frozen.

migration.description: RFC-004 (Draft): optional migration metadata block. Audit-only — no migration tooling ships with the strict schema. Strict on v1 frozen fields; extension fields permitted.

context_cost.description: Research/benchmark track (benchmarks/context_cost/RFC.md). No spec semantics depend on this field. Permissive.

gaming_profile.description: R4-P1-4 gaming.klickd baseline (preview, registry-based). NOT a v4 MUST. Accepted as a registry-defined optional profile; permissive structure.

deprecated_fields.description: OPTIONAL informational block defined by docs/spec/DEPRECATION_POLICY_V4.md §6. Lists fields the producer has emitted as 'deprecated' status. Readers that don't understand it MUST silently ignore (§33.7).

_example_metadata.description: Optional non-normative block used by repository examples (see examples/v4/personas/README.md). Carries 'persona', 'non_normative', 'contains_real_pii', 'contains_secrets', etc. A reader MUST NOT use this block as a trust signal.

gateLevel.description: RFC-002 v1 §6 — fixed enum of five gate levels. No new levels are added in GA.

mediaProfileEntry.description: RFC-001 v1 entry. Strict on v1 frozen fields (id, modality, hash). Additional metadata (consent, producer, etc.) is permitted as extension surface.

modality.description: RFC-001 v1 §4 #3 — closed enum at v1. Custom modalities live under 'x_*' namespaces (outside this entry).

uri.description: May be file://, https://, ipfs://, cas://<hash>, or a relative path. Reader chooses how to resolve.

bytes_b64.description: Inline base64 PERMITTED ONLY for entries ≤ 16 KiB total (RFC-001 v1 §4 #1). Strict enforcement of the 16 KiB cap is not expressible in JSON Schema and is enforced by reader-side logic.

algo.description: RFC-001 v1 §4 #2 — one hash, one algorithm: BLAKE3.

value.description: Hash value, encoding per RFC-001 (base64 or hex-prefixed). Strict format enforcement is reader-side.

consent.description: RFC-001 v1 §4 #5 — consent is per-entry, not per-file.
